skill.name: nature-paper-to-patent
skill.description: Convert scientific papers, theses, technical reports, source code, figures, or research manuscripts into evidence-grounded Chinese invention patent drafts. Use when an AI agent must extract patentable technical contributions, map every claimed feature to source evidence, preserve core formulas as editable Office Math, generate claim-aligned flowcharts and methodology figures, compare a paper with an existing patent, audit support and consistency, or deliver separate Chinese DOCX files for claims, specification, abstract, and abstract figure.
skill.summary: Convert research papers into evidence-grounded Chinese invention patent drafts with traceable claims, editable Office Math formulas, and validated DOCX deliverables.
skill.author: Yuan1z0825

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 291 potential issues across 34 files, but the vast majority are false positives from regex pattern matching on documentation and standard library usage. All 122 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are false positives matching on Python `hashlib` imports for content hashing and on the word 'hash' in regex patterns. The 154 'external_commands' alerts are markdown backtick characters (code formatting) in README and reference files, not actual command execution. The 5 'shutil operations' are legitimate file copying for project workspace initialization. The 1 `subprocess.run` call in build_patent_package.py uses hardcoded script paths with no user input injection vector. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized network activity was found.

low_findings.title: Subprocess invocation of companion scripts
low_findings.description: build_patent_package.py uses subprocess.run to invoke render_patent_docx.py and render_flowchart_svg.py with arguments derived from user-provided paths. Arguments are validated Path objects, not shell strings, so command injection risk is minimal.

content.user_title: Convert Research Papers into Chinese Patent Drafts
content.value_statement: Converting a research paper into a Chinese invention patent draft requires tracing every claim feature back to source evidence and preserving formulas as editable math. This skill provides a structured workflow with validation gates that produce a complete, evidence-grounded Chinese patent application package.
actual_capabilities: Extracts searchable text from PDF papers using pypdf with OCR-needed detection
Builds a structured draft JSON with source map, evidence ledger, claim-feature map, and formula inventory
Validates draft completeness with quality thresholds for evidence support, claim architecture, and terminology consistency
Renders core equations as native editable Office Math (OMML) in DOCX output
Generates claim-aligned SVG flowcharts and methodology figures in black-and-white patent style
Produces separate Chinese DOCX files for claims (权利要求书), specification (说明书), abstract (说明书摘要), and abstract figure (摘要附图)
limitations: Does not provide patentability, infringement, or filing-guarantee opinions (explicitly stated in SKILL.md)
Cannot infer inventorship, ownership, publication dates, or prior-art conclusions without explicit confirmation
OCR for scanned PDFs is not included; the skill flags low-text PDFs and recommends external OCR
Output is a drafting aid for inventor and patent-professional review, not a filed application

use_cases.title: University researcher drafting a Chinese patent
use_cases.description: A PhD student has a published paper on a new defect-detection algorithm and wants to file a Chinese invention patent. The skill extracts the paper, maps every claim feature to source pages, and produces a validated DOCX package.

use_cases.title: Patent agent auditing a paper-patent relationship
use_cases.description: A patent agent receives a client paper and an existing granted patent. The skill performs a bidirectional paper-patent audit, identifying paper-only, patent-only, broader, and narrower claim features with exact locators.

use_cases.title: R&D engineer preparing a disclosure analysis
use_cases.description: A corporate R&D engineer has a technical report and source code. The skill produces a disclosure analysis extracting patentable contributions with formula preservation and evidence-grounded support states.
use_cases.target_user: Corporate R&D engineers and technology transfer teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF to Chinese patent draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the paper at paper/main.pdf into a complete Chinese invention patent application. Use full-draft mode with algorithm-software invention type. Produce separate DOCX files for claims, specification, abstract, and abstract figure.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a single PDF paper

prompt_templates.title: Scanned PDF with OCR uncertainty
prompt_templates.prompt: The paper is a scanned PDF at paper/scanned.pdf with some formulas unclear after OCR. Generate a claim draft for the confirmed technical solution only. Do not guess uncertain equation symbols and mark them with [TO CONFIRM].
prompt_templates.scenario: User with low-quality or scanned PDF needing careful handling

prompt_templates.title: Paper-patent audit comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare paper/new-method.pdf with existing-patent/cn-grant-2023.pdf. For each claim in the existing patent, determine if the paper provides support. Label items as paper-only, patent-only, patent-broader, or patent-narrower with exact locators.
prompt_templates.scenario: User performing prior-art or freedom-to-operate analysis

prompt_templates.title: Mixed project with source code and figures
prompt_templates.prompt: The workspace contains a paper PDF, supplementary source code in supplementary/source-code/, and source figures in source-figures/. Use mixed-project mode. Include code-derived evidence with C-prefixed source IDs and preserve algorithm pseudocode in the specification.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with multi-format research disclosure

output_examples.input: PDF paper on attention-based defect detection with model architecture, loss function, and three-stage pipeline.
output: Structured draft JSON with source map (P001-P045), equation inventory (E001-E008), figure inventory (F001-F006)
Evidence ledger with 20+ entries tagged explicit, inherent, or needs-confirmation
Independent claim 1 covering the three-stage pipeline mapped to P012, P023, E003
Five DOCX files: 权利要求书.docx, 说明书.docx, 说明书摘要.docx, 摘要附图.docx, 完整审阅稿.docx
Validation report passing all quality thresholds (evidence_support=5, claim_architecture=5)

output_examples.input: Scanned PDF where equations on pages 7-9 are partially garbled by OCR.
output: Claim draft with only OCR-confirmed technical features
Three equation slots marked [TO CONFIRM: symbol identity on page 8, equation 3]
Inventor question list with specific OCR uncertainties
Validation report showing CLAIM_PLACEHOLDER warnings for unconfirmed features

best_practices: Always state the detected source_format, task_mode, and invention_type axes before loading fragments to avoid loading irrelevant reference files
Create stable source IDs (P001, E001, F001, C001) before drafting so every claim feature can be traced back to evidence
Run validate_patent_draft.py after populating the structured draft and resolve all ERROR findings before building the final package
Label uncertain content with [TO CONFIRM: specific question] outside formal claims to keep claims clean and legally defensible
anti_patterns: Do not draft the patent application directly from the paper abstract or contribution list without building the full source map first
Do not include unsupported features (status=unsupported) in formal claims; they must be excluded or confirmed with the inventor
Do not use plain LaTeX strings as visible formulas in the DOCX output; always render as native Office Math for editability
Do not infer inventorship, ownership, or publication dates; these require explicit human confirmation and legal review

faq.question: What input formats does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports four source_format values: selectable-text PDF, scanned PDF (OCR flag), pasted text, and mixed project with supplementary code and figures. The detect logic in manifest.yaml routes to the correct fragment.

faq.question: Does the skill produce a filed patent application?
faq.answer: No. The output is explicitly a drafting aid for inventor and patent-professional review. SKILL.md states it is not a patentability opinion, infringement opinion, or filing guarantee.

faq.question: How are formulas handled in the DOCX output?
faq.answer: Core formulas are converted from LaTeX to native Office Math (OMML) using math_to_omml.py, making them fully editable in Word. The skill validates that every equation has a LaTeX source, source IDs, symbol definitions, and a technical role.

faq.question: What does the validation script check?
faq.answer: validate_patent_draft.py checks source ID format, claim numbering continuity, evidence-to-claim mapping, formula coverage, figure alignment, quality score thresholds, and forbidden alias usage. It returns ERROR or WARNING findings with a report file.

faq.question: Can the skill compare a paper with an existing patent?
faq.answer: Yes. Use paper-patent-audit task mode. The skill produces separate source maps for the paper and the existing patent, then builds a bidirectional feature table with labels like paper-only, patent-only, patent-broader, and patent-narrower.

faq.question: What Python dependencies are required?
faq.answer: The skill requires latex2mathml (>=3.77.0), Pillow (>=10.0.0), pypdf (>=4.0.0), and python-docx (>=1.1.0). These are listed in requirements.txt and cover PDF text extraction, math conversion, image handling, and DOCX generation.
